what_it_does: A React hook that manages open/close state for a chip-triggered menu and, on each open, measures the chip wrapper's screen position to decide whether the menu should be right-anchored so it does not overflow the viewport's right edge.
observations: Uses useState for open and menuClass, useRef for wrapRef, and useCallback for toggle and close.
On open, getBoundingClientRect is read from wrapRef.current and r.left + menuW is compared against window.innerWidth - 8.
If overflow is predicted, the CSS modifier class lenschip-menu--right is set; otherwise the class is cleared.
The default assumed menu width is 240px and can be overridden via menuW.
The close callback simply sets open to false.
standouts: Collision detection is performed only at open time using the chip wrapper's current viewport position.
A single CSS modifier class encodes the anchoring decision rather than inline styles.
The hook returns both the state handlers and the DOM ref needed for measurement.
conventions: Follows the React useHook naming convention.
Uses functional update form of setOpen to compute the next class inside the toggle.
Assumes an absolute-positioned menu with left:0 by default and a right-anchored variant via modifier class.
concerns: window.innerWidth is accessed directly inside the toggle with no guard for non-browser environments, although it is only reached after a DOM ref exists.
The menu width is assumed via parameter rather than measured from the rendered menu.
No repositioning happens while the menu is open, so viewport changes after open are not handled.
Only right-edge overflow is checked; left, top, and bottom clipping are not addressed.
setMenuClass is called inside the setOpen functional updater, which is a side effect in a state reducer.
notes: Small, file-local hook that centralizes chip-menu toggle state and a right-edge overflow flip for an absolutely positioned popover.
questions: How is the lenschip-menu--right class styled in CSS?
Are there call sites that need bottom or left-edge collision handling as well?
Is the actual rendered menu width guaranteed to match the menuW default/argument?
How do the three call sites bind wrapRef to the chip wrapper?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is shared chip-menu state plus popover collision handling, and the file implements exactly that.
intention_signals: The idea is to give chip menus a single reusable source of toggle state and a lightweight right-edge collision guard, avoiding duplicated per-chip logic.
novelty: The right-edge anchoring decision is computed fresh from the chip's bounding rectangle each time the menu opens, using a configurable assumed menu width.
contribution: Provides a reusable open/close state manager and a viewport-overflow guard for chip menus, exporting a wrapper ref and a modifier class that a consumer can attach to its chip and menu markup.
summary_for_embedding: useChipMenu is a React hook that manages chip menu open state and toggles a right-anchored CSS modifier when the menu would overflow the viewport's right edge.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to centralize chip menu toggle behavior and right-edge collision handling for optics chip popovers.

symbols.does: Manages chip menu open state and applies a right-anchored modifier class when the menu would overflow the viewport right edge.
symbols.description: useChipMenu is a custom React hook that tracks whether a chip menu is open, exposes a toggle and close callback, and holds a ref to the chip wrapper. When toggling open, it measures the wrapper's bounding rectangle and compares the expected menu width against the viewport right edge with an 8px margin, setting the class lenschip-menu--right when the menu needs to be anchored to the right instead of the default left.
